seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with Mastra + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with Mastra + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with Mastra enables powerful code-aware agents that analyze repositories, execute in sandboxes, validate tests, and debug iteratively. However, without proper guardrails, these agents risk generating insecure code, leaking credentials, or failing silently due to unvalidated workflow transitions. Vex integration as middleware ensures reliability by verifying code correctness, security, and compliance at every step.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with Mastra
architectureOverview.description:
The Mastra-based AI Coding Assistant follows a workflow-driven architecture where an LLM agent receives repository context via knowledge retrieval, generates code proposals, executes them in a sandbox, validates tests, and iterates on debugging. Mastra's workflow steps handle transitions between analysis, generation, execution, and validation phases, with memory managing conversation history and repo state. Third-party integrations pull git repos and run security scans.

Key components include Mastra's AgentExecutor for LLM orchestration, KnowledgeBase for repo context retrieval, and custom tools for sandbox execution (e.g., Docker-in-Docker) and test runners (e.g., Jest/Pytest). Vex middleware intercepts outputs between steps to validate code artifacts before progression.

This setup scales to handle multiple repos simultaneously but introduces risks around stale context injection and malformed tool responses.

risks.name: Workflow step outputs not validated between transitions
risks.description: Mastra workflows pass unverified code between generation, execution, and validation steps, allowing malformed outputs to propagate.
risks.mitigation: Vex middleware validates JSON schemas and code syntax at each transition point.

risks.name: Agent tool calls returning malformed data
risks.description: Sandbox execution tools return inconsistent formats, confusing the LLM during iterative debugging.
risks.mitigation: Standardize tool outputs with Vex response validation before agent consumption.

risks.name: Memory retrieval injecting stale context
risks.description: KnowledgeBase retrieves outdated repo snapshots, causing agents to debug non-existent code.
risks.mitigation: Vex freshness checks on retrieved context with repo commit validation.

risks.name: Sync integration data causing agent confusion
risks.description: Third-party git integrations deliver partial repo states, leading to incomplete code analysis.
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies integration completeness before workflow progression.

implementation.title: Mastra Workflow with Coding Agent
implementation.description: Define the core workflow that orchestrates code analysis, generation, execution, and validation.

implementation.title: Repository Context Retrieval
implementation.description: KnowledgeBase integration pulls relevant code files with commit freshness.

implementation.title: Sandbox Execution Tool
implementation.description: Secure code execution with test validation in isolated environment.

implementation.title: Iterative Debugging Loop
implementation.description: Workflow loop that continues until tests pass or max iterations reached.

vexIntegration.title: Vex Middleware in Mastra Pipeline
vexIntegration.description: Wrap Mastra workflow with Vex validation at critical transition points.

vexIntegration.title: Code Correctness & Security Check
vexIntegration.description: Vex policy suite for verifying generated code meets quality standards.

vexIntegration.title: Context Freshness Validation
vexIntegration.description: Ensure repo context is current before agent processing.

cta.heading: Secure Your Mastra AI Coding Assistant
cta.description: Add Vex middleware today to eliminate code generation risks and ensure production-ready reliability.
